source.title: Information theoretic MPC for model-based reinforcement learning
source.venue: 2017 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation

confidence_rationale: Information-theoretic MPC provides a close sampling-based control analogue in which trajectory weights and entropy/control-cost structure matter, rather than only hard elite survival. This supports using entropy/support diagnostics and weak bias dose-response measurements. It is not direct evidence for changing REE's CEM implementation to MPPI.
failure_signatures: A hard elite-only planner can erase lower-ranked alternatives before downstream biases have a chance to act.
If the score range dwarfs the bias range, a support surface may exist but remain behaviourally immovable under naturalistic bias magnitudes.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Information-theoretic MPC derives sampling-based model-predictive control schemes for stochastic optimal control and compares them with CEM-style MPC.
mapping.ree_translation: For REE, the paper supports the diagnostic stance that proposal support and score/bias scale have to be measured together. V3-EXQ-563b should therefore record candidate entropy, E3 score range, score_bias range, and selected-rank changes before and after bias.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper is an MPC/control paper, not a hippocampal or CEM-support-preservation paper. It motivates entropy-weighted thinking and dose-response diagnostics; it does not justify replacing REE CEM with MPPI in this work package.
mapping.source_context: Robotics/control paper using sampling-based MPC for model-based RL with neural-network dynamics and nonlinear costs.

confidence_components.notes: Useful as an entropy/control-cost analogue. The direct repair remains CEM-local rather than a planner swap.
